summary.intent: Address a Codex automated review pass on PR #681 (ars-ui repo) by resolving five existing threads, pushing a fix, and iterating until the next review pass completed cleanly.
summary.outcome: Three new Codex review findings were addressed with regression tests and a production fix to TimeField buffer validation; all verification checks passed and the fix was committed and pushed.

repo: The project uses a `waiting-for-codex-review` skill in `.agents/skills/` to manage the Codex review loop protocol.
Codex signals active review via a 👀 reaction on the PR and drops it when done; absence of the reaction plus new unresolved threads signals a completed pass with findings.

The verification stack (no mutation tests) is: cargo test --lib, spec conformance tests, insta snapshot tests, cargo xtask spec validate, nightly fmt --check, clippy -D warnings, and an --ignored proptest.
The project uses `cargo insta` for snapshot testing and `cargo xtask spec validate` for spec conformance validation.
Commits require a co-author trailer for Codex: `Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>`.

code.finding: TypeBufferCommit / finalization logic for numeric segments had a bug: a below-min value (e.g., hour 0 below minimum) was not being clamped/published correctly on blur.
Three regression tests were added: empty day-period blur must not publish, below-min hour 0 must not clamp/publish incorrectly, and combining-mark-only CJK input must be ignored.
Spec pseudocode around bounds checks and buffer commit for TypeBufferCommit / CJK day-period prefix handling was tightened to match the corrected implementation.

workflow: The Codex review loop protocol is: push → apply snapshot label → reply and resolve all Codex threads → post @codex review trigger → poll reactions/CI at 2-minute intervals until 👀 drops.
Mutation tests are explicitly excluded from the focused verification stack during this review iteration cycle.
Tests should be written red-first against the current code before applying the production fix, to confirm they actually exercise the bug.
CI matrix order observed: snapshot → formatting → clippy → workspace check → mutual exclusion → unit tests → integration → adapters → coverage → browser → feature-flag groups → release verification.

friction: The Codex review pass took significantly longer than earlier passes, requiring many 2-minute polling intervals (roughly 20+ minutes of waiting) before findings appeared.
GraphQL query for unresolved Codex threads had to be constructed manually and run via `gh api graphql` with inline query strings, which is verbose and fragile.
The five threads from the prior pass all needed individual reply + resolve calls with no batch API, adding sequential latency.
open_items: Mutation tests were intentionally skipped throughout this session and have not been run against the new fix.
CI run 26416150139 was still in progress at the time of the final commit; full CI green confirmation on the new commit is pending.
A fresh `@codex review` pass has not yet been triggered for the new commit addressing the three findings.
